name: SISU Racing Endurance Ride
description: SISU Racing is one of Zwift’s most prominent and respected racing teams, known for grit, consistency, and a strong community. Join us for our weekly endurance ride designed to help you build fitness the right way. Ride at your own pace, stay steady, and log quality miles while a SISU Racing ride leader keeps the group motivated. Whether you’re training for racing, events, or long-term fitness, this is the perfect way to grow endurance, confidence, and resilience—together. Join us and put in the work, SISU style.  Join the conversation on our Zwift Ride Radio in the SISU Racing Discord - link available at sisu.racing.  Let's go riding!

name: EVOLVE 50km Road Race
description:
Join Team Evolve for the EVOLVE 50, an electrifying 50km race on Zwift, utilizing the Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) system to match cyclists of all levels in a dynamic, community-driven virtual peloton. 

This Category Enforced event requires a smart trainer/power meter and heart rate monitor, grouping riders by their ZRS (0-1000 scale), fostering a competitive yet fair atmosphere.

ZRS FAQ: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

About Us
At Team Evolve, we are more than just a group of athletes we are a dynamic and inclusive community united by our passion for a wide range of activities, both virtual and outdoor. 

Whether you’re into structured training, social rides, running, swimming, or simply staying active in any form, you’ll discover a welcoming and supportive environment here.

For more information visit our website www.teamevolve.cc and join us on our Discord Server: https://discord.gg/teamevolve

name: Invictus Games Foundation Sunday Social
description:
Hosted by the Invictus Games Foundation. Below the link to the sign up for the event:

https://reg.resport.io/s/step1.jsp?r=4747885851705344

Join our social ride led by members of the Invictus Games international community of serving military personnel and veterans who use cycling to recover from injury.

A sub-2.0 w/kg (Cat D) weekly group ride with the goal of keeping the group together. This event is open to any ability including those who use handcycles, recumbents, trikes, tandems and standard bikes.

Follow the ride leader's instructions and stay as close to the beacon as possible. If you're feeling strong, please use your abilities to help pull fellow riders back to the pack.

There will be inspirational chat and a fun, social atmosphere for your training. Please ask questions - about using sport for recovery, training, set-up and the experiences of our Invictus family.

It is not a race. Use the draft.

Late joiners allowed.

If you are a member of the WIS community and happy to let the group know, please write [*IGF*] after your name on your profile.
For more information about the Invictus Games Foundation please go to https://invictusgamesfoundation.org/

name: GXY INFINITY RAMP!  [ 2.5 ~ 3.2 WKG] Cat C
description:
Welcome to our GXY INFINITY ramp group ride!

This session is all about taking you through your zonal power zones.

After the warm-up, we start with tempo efforts continuing up to your threshold and
above on the final lap or climb dependent on the course.

In the interest of fairness and structure, the fence will remain live until any
final climb or push to the finish line.

GXY is a friendly and supportive club who strives to help others succeed. We are
looking forward to riding with you.

Please join in with the banter and fun by using on-screen messaging or using our
dedicated INFINITY discord channel( link below)

If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or
both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link.

[https://discord.gg/UqnJMM3jd6]

https://discord.gg/UqnJMM3jd6 [https://discord.gg/UqnJMM3jd6]

eventSubgroups.name: GXY INFINITY RAMP!  [ 2.5 ~ 3.2 WKG] Cat C (C)

description:
Sunday Session with Team Giant
 
Amp up your training every Sunday with Giant ambassadors and athletes. Join riders from around the world on a training pace at 2.7 - 3.0 W/kg, and get an exclusive opportunity to connect with fellow riders as well as Giant pros and community leaders.
 
 
 
#GiantBicycles #sundaysession

name: Women's Mini Races || p/b FemmeCycle Collab
description:
Join the FemmeCyle Collab on Sundays for mass start mini races. Each mini race is a scratch race which should take anywhere from 5-20 minutes. You'll be able to see all the women entered in the race, but results will be listed by racing score category.  Try to stay with the women of your category or the higher categories if you can for these short fun races!

Zwift Club: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/260d0d7e-4b91-4162-b025-fd202b8a6e74/home

name: Epic Run Crew: Sunday Scoot
description:
This is Epic Run Crew’s weekly long run. We will change the route and distance periodically, to keep the run fresh and fun. Most weeks we will run 15K, but if you don’t want to run that far, come join us for 5 or 10 K. You decide.

Epic Run Crew is based in Vancouver, BC, Canada. We were founded as an in-person club in 2021 and then joined Zwift in June 2022.

Use the Zwift Companion app to join the Epic Run Crew club, for access to exclusive member-only group runs.

Facebook (group): https://www.facebook.com/groups/1210113516293815
Facebook (page): https://www.facebook.com/epicruncrew2
Instagram: @epicruncrew

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:

Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: The Elle Word
description:
What is The “Elle” Word? At PUMP, it means women riding together. Join ride leader Elle for a casual 45–60 minute group ride built around steady miles and supportive company. Riders are encouraged to settle into their own rhythm while the main group typically rides at a comfortable Zone 2 pace.

The Elle Word is about showing up, riding together, and building fitness in a friendly and supportive environment. Along the way there may be the occasional QOM segment or sprint effort for anyone who wants to stretch the legs and have a little fun before settling back into the group.

Strong legs strong community. That’s The Elle Word.

name: Seattle Baby Steps 1.0-1.3 w/kg
description:
Are you looking for a supportive beginner or recovery ride?  If so welcome to Seattle Baby Steps! This weekly ride will be paced at an average of 1.0-1.3 w/kg on the flats and is intended for riders who are newer, younger, older, and recovering from injuries.  This is an enjoyable and conversational social ride where your participation is encouraged.  Baby Steps' goal is to help you build strength and confidence to try the hundreds of advanced rides, races, and events that are available every week on Zwift.

We haven't jumped on to the Discord bandwagon yet but join us on our Facebook page at: Seattle Baby Steps - Weekly Zwift Group Ride to stay in touch with the group and to share your thoughts and ask Zwift and other cycling-related questions. https://www.facebook.com/search/top/?q=seattle%20baby%20steps%20-%20weekly%20zwift%20group%20ride

GROUP RIDE ETIQUETTE FOR NEWCOMERS

Please respect the ride leader's pace and instructions. This is not a race and the ride leader will not chase anyone riding off the front of the group. If you feel the need to stretch your legs, do so on the sprints and then prepare to regroup immediately afterward. Faster riders are encouraged to sweep in the back to assist less strong riders who may be struggling to rejoin the group after sprints and inclines. Sprinkle Ride Ons far and wide to encourage and delight your fellow riders! Join in on the conversation (on-screen text), but be mindful of the younger folks (i.e., children) who join the ride from time to time.

name: NoPinz R3R - 60km Race
description:
Welcome to this NoPinz R3R Race. The rules are simple....
Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.
Cats A, and B will require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor.
Cat C will require power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor for podium spots.
Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual (power) record and to make analysis publicly available on ZwiftPower; they must also be using a direct drive trainer or power meter.
**Elite level performances are also expected to be supported by dual (power) recordings from reputable power sources or easily verifiable outside data. This applies to those with a history of such performances.
** 5.2 w/kg (20 mins); 6 w/kg (5 mins); 9.8 w/kg (1 min)
Zwift Activities must be saved publicly.
Please also maintain an open and active Strava profile to facilitate organiser inquiry.
Please see http://www.zwiftpower.com for finalised race results. The race is brought to you by NoPinz please visit https://nopinz.com/product-tag/subzero/ All subzero kit can be custom made in any design.

name: Weekend Long Run
description:
Join your fellow Zwift Runners for a long tempo run designed to prepare you for those early season half-marathons. Pick a pace group and let them push you or play the role of pacer to help out everyone else.

Everyone is welcome. Warm-up on your own and finish with tempo here or use this to set yourself up for a longer run after with some other runners.

B group - 10mi/16.1km
C group - 9mi/14.5km
D group - 7mi/11.3km

name: Black Cyclists Network Group Ride
description: Join The Black Cyclists Network (BCN) every Sunday at 15:30 UTC for a social ride with a little competitive Hare and Hound PURGE at the end. Led by BCN Ride Captains. We welcome all skill levels. We will ride at 2.5Watt/KG and below pace so no matter where in the world you are, let’s ride together as members of the Zwift community. RIDE ON!

name: OTR Snap Crackle Pop Punch Time
description:
Snap Crackle Pop Punch Time is a group ride, race & interval training all rolled into one fun challenging event. Unique “race” scoring, where results in ZwiftPower are based on sum of segment times on a route with punchy climbs and/or sprints. Group rides together at ~2wkg, and ride leader provides guidance and lead-outs to smash a segment; then regroup, rinse & repeat. New route every week.

Multiple time slots on Fri-Sun, including Fri Atlantic, Sun APAC and Sun Americas/EMEA.
Riders need to join ZwiftPower.com to see their results as sum of segment times.

Event links: https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/snapcracklepop
Weekly results (across all time slots): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=2656
In-ride conversation (“SCP Ride” channel): https://discord.gg/B52qdExaW2
Chat, banter and results: https://discord.gg/HNzvx98srF

Group ride with all cats together at ~2w/kg. Yellow beacon / ride leader (when present) will provide instructions for each segment, and drop the fence when it's time to punch/sprint. Fence is for guidance only (non-zapping).

Ride at a relaxed pace between the timed segments.
Work together to optimize draft for faster segment times.
Slow or stop with yellow beacon after each segment to regroup.

All categories start in a single pen.
Results split per WKG category in ZwiftPower.
No power ups, default drafting, no TT bikes.

One of our regular SCP routes is 3.5 laps of Innsbruckring, with 4x Leg Snappers plus 3x sprints (stopping to regroup on the kicker in between). The challenge is to achieve sub 4 mins for the sum of 7 segment times for cats A/B (that's 45 secs per snapper plus 20 secs per sprint), sub 5 mins for cat C, and sub 7 mins for cat D. Best time to beat is 3:33.
Historical results for Leg Snapper: https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1322

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO race series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: USMeS Ridgeline Assault 2.5–5.0 w/kg
description:
Welcome to USMeS Ridgeline Assault...where Sundays are earned one climb at a time.
This is a climbing-focused ride built for riders who want sustained elevation, steady pressure, and the freedom to push hard when the road tilts up. Routes change week to week, but the mission stays the same...commit to the climb and see what you’ve got.

Pace: 2.5–5.0 w/kg (choose your effort)
Duration: 60 minutes
2 Yellow Beacons
⚡️ Lead Beacon - spicier pace off the front
⚡️ Secondary beacon — pacing the advertised effort
🚫 No fence — ride where it makes sense for you

Ride Format
• Climb heavy routes with sustained efforts on the ascents
• A focused, supportive environment where hard work is expected

Expectations
• Climbing is honest work.
• Respect all riders, ride your pace, and stay engaged.
• Every summit counts, and every effort adds up.

When the climbing is done, spin it out and recover with the Sunday Endurance Ride. The perfect way to reset after the assault.
Find your line. Hold it to the top.

#conqueringlimits
#challengeaccepted

name: REVO Social SUB2
description: Join us for a 1.5-2 w/kg paced ride on a flat course. Let's stick together using the banded group ride feature! This allows everyone to stay together and chat while still doing your own effort, whether that’s truly a SUB2 or you want to do some intervals along the way. Complete this ride and unlock our Revolution Velo kit! Hosted by Revolution Velo Racing, a Zwift team for women. To hear about our upcoming rides, check out our Facebook Fans page at https://www.facebook.com/groups/RevoFans/

eventSubgroups.name: REVO Social SUB2 (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
Join us for a 1.5-2 w/kg paced ride on a flat course. Let's stick together using the new banded group ride feature as a trial this week! We will not chase riders that go off the front, and the ride lead may use the fence to keep everyone together. If you're feeling strong, please use your abilities and drop back and help pull the slower riders back up to the pack if someone gets dropped.

Complete this ride and unlock our Revolution Velo kit! Hosted by Revolution Velo Racing, a Zwift team for women. To hear about our upcoming rides, check out our Facebook Fans page at https://www.facebook.com/groups/RevoFans/

#revo

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (TSE)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by TSE (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ccc82468-4cd1-4bdf-abbd-6a35ae8ae518).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

eventSeries.name: ZRL 25/26 R4
eventSeries.description: Zwift Racing League Season 20 (2025/26 - Round 4). Includes all races and official recon rides

name: Velos Bumper Chase Race
description:
'Bumper' races prepare horses for the biggest events, so why not cyclists? And the name 'Chase' means 'to hunt' in old French, so join Velos for our newest event: 'The Bumper' Chase Race! We change courses weekly to keep it fresh, some weeks flat & others hilly... something for every taste.

A chase-style race is loads of fun because you work with others in your CAT to try and stay ahead of the faster riders. Wait for your group’s horn to sound: Ds take off first, then staggered starts release the Cs, Bs, and As from their pens with increasingly harder efforts required. If the group behind catches you, try to work with them to the finish. Each CAT wears a different kit, making it easier to see the group you are working to catch. Chase races help you learn how to work WITH a team, since a tighter bunch means a faster group. You will master team racing skills and get stronger whilst having fun along the way: the winning formula for ALL Velos events!

Join us at teamvelos.co.uk

name: Pride Run
description:
Join with members of the LGBTQ+ community as we stride with Pride! 

Runs are hosted by LGBTQ Zwifters Club and we invite you to join our weekly rainbow run! In some runs you will find a leader to make it social and help keep you motivated. If you are in training for a duathlon or triathlon, you can even join a Pride Ride before your run, since runs are scheduled 30 min after each Pride ride ends. Our runs are inclusive, we value diversity and courage, and we are always respectful. Social chat may involve LGBTQ+ topics so please come with an open heart and open mind. 

If you want to learn more about our Club and socials, please visit: https://lgbtq-zwifters.com/

name: The Herd Rode to Recovery
description:
New to Zwift, just a little out of shape or recovering from injury? This is the group ride for you! All are welcome to join The Herd on this supportive and social event.

Respect the leader's pace and ride with the yellow beacon. This is NOT A RACE! Stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help sweep the riders struggling in the back.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV (Please use Push-To-Talk)
Check us out on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/zHerd/

#herd

name: Cycle Nation Sunday Bumble
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Sunday Bumble Ride. Join us for an easy evening social ride.

Your co-operation is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the ride leader (yellow beacon).

This is not a race and we encourage you to stay with the ride leader (yellow beacon) and if you are feeling strong you are more than welcome to assist with sweeping duties at the back of the ride helping dropped riders rejoin the group. We're looking forward to hosting you for the a ride.

Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM

name: Sunday Masters Trilogy (1/3) - Team INOX
description:
⚡️🚴🏼‍💨 INOX Masters Trilogy  by Team INOX ⚡️🚴🏼‍💨
• You must have an active Zwift Power account on https://zwiftpower.com
• Heart rate monitors are mandatory for podium positions
• Smart trainers or power meters must be connected as the power source to participate in races
• Enter the correct age category
Make sure you meet all requirements to avoid disqualification

INOX Masters Trilogy will have a double weekly appointment: Saturday and Sunday; in which three races will be run every 20 minutes
To ensure balanced and fair competition the starts will be simultaneous with the cyclists visible to each other categories and the 5 ages start categories will be:
Elite (Cat A) → Up to 29 years old
M30 (Cat B) → 30 to 39 years old
M40 (Cat C) → 40 to 49 years old
M50 (Cat D) → 50 to 59 years old
M60+ (Cat E) → 60 years old and up

Race Type: SCRATCH
Bike Upgrade Enabled
Steering Enabled
Power-Ups: NONE
Jersey Unlock: FREESPEED

Every day of race will have a ranking time of all three races
The ranking will be divided into 10 categories based on the age groups:
A = Under 35
B = 35 - 39
C = 40 - 44
D = 45 - 49
E = 50 - 54
F = 55- 59
G = 60 -64
H = 65 - 69
I = 70 - 74
J = Over 75

More info will be communicated promptly via the Discord and Facebook channels
The organizers reserve the right to carry out checks in case of suspected misconduct and if necessary cancel the performances obtained

CONTACT US
• Website: https://www.teaminox.it/
• Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/inoxteam/
• Zwift Companion Club: https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/cef70cde...44703/home
• Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cpr3rBGaZy
• ZwiftPower: https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=16461
• STRAVA: https://strava.app.link/p2Oj6TaE3Gb

Everyone is Welcome! 🙏🏻

Come and hang out with Team Rave.

The yellow beacon will cruise at 2w/kg - 2.5w/kg.
The red beacon at 1.8w/kg - 2w/kg.

name: BC Bike Race Sunday Endurance Workout
description:
Since 2007, athletes from 65 different countries have been racing the Ultimate Singletrack Adventure in British Columbia, Canada and now, BC Bike Race is bringing you a new experience to Zwift.
The BC Bike Race Endurance Workout is a weekly ride designed to help build your endurance during the winter months in preparation for the summer to shred singletrack.
For more information please visit bcbikerace.com

eventSubgroups.name: BC Bike Race Sunday Endurance Workout (E)

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Group Run 10k
description:
Join us for a 10k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends. #pbt

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: GC Ride - Recovery & Social
description:
GC Ride – German Cycling Community Ride
Fahre gemeinsam mit der German Cycling Community auf Zwift!
Der GC Ride ist eine offene Gruppenfahrt für alle, die gemeinsam Rad fahren möchten.
🚴 Offen für alle Fahrerinnen und Fahrer
🚴 Kein Rennen – entspanntes Gruppentempo
🚴 Gemeinschaft und Spaß am Radfahren
Die GC Rides verbinden die digitale Zwift-Community mit dem Breitensport von German Cycling.
Der Donnerstag bleibt die offizielle vRTF-Wertungsfahrt von German Cycling.
Die GC Rides stehen für Motivation, Austausch und gemeinsames Fahren.

name: ZTPL Cycling Club Coffee Ride
description:
☀️Weszliśmy w sezon wiosenno-letni.☀️
Część naszych eventów zostaje w kalendarzu, ale może zdarzyć się tak że nie będzie na nich naszych liderów jazd.
Jeśli tak się zdarzy, gorąca prośba do Was o jazdę wg. założeń danego eventu.
Nasze eventy i liderzy ZTPL Cycling Club wrócą ponownie na jesień 2026.
Póki co RIDE ON i korzystajcie z pogody, a jeśli nie możecie to bawcie się dobrze podczas naszych eventów.

Zapraszamy na wspólny event z ZTPL Cycling Club. Pamiętajcie o wyborze właściwej grupy.
Z założenia nasze jazdy prowadzą liderzy, którzy pomagają trzymać peleton w ryzach. Jeśli podczas eventu zabraknie lidera, starajcie się trzymać tempo na które się umówiliśmy w momencie gdy dołączyliście do wybranej grupy. To od Was zależy jak będziecie się bawić 😊

Po ukończonym evencie, jeśli jechaliście z nami pierwszy raz, w Waszym wirtualnym garażu będzie czekała nasza teamowa koszulka!
◾️Grupa E jest grupą dla najbardziej początkujących. Tempo będzie dostojne 😊 i będzie oscylowało w okolicach 1.3-1.7 w/kg
◾️Grupa D jest grupą początkującą. Tempo jest spokojne, uczymy się jazdy w grupie, jest czas żeby porozmawiać. Tempo będzie nieco wyższe niż w grupie E w granicach 1.8-2.5 w/kg
◾️ Grupa C przeznaczona jest dla średnio zaawansowanych. Tempo będzie oscylować w okolicach 2.7-3.2 w/kg z przodu peletonu (możliwe akcenty).
Ważne: Jeśli wasze FTP jest w granicach 2.5w/kg to nie będzie grupa dla Was
◾️ Jeśli chcecie przejechać jazdę w tempie Coffee Ride wybierzcie o jedną grupę niżej niż sugeruje wam wasze FTP lub zwiftpower :)
◾️ Wartości podane w opisach dotyczą średniego tempa z CAŁEJ jazdy (nie uwzględniają interwałów, które mogą wystąpić podczas np. podjazdów)
◾️ PAMIĘTAJCIE że podczas jazd grupowych NIE UŻYWAMY ROWERÓW CZASOWYCH!
◾️ Starajcie się pilnować peletonu i nie zgubić koła, samemu bardzo trudno jest odrobić stratę.
◾️ Proszę słuchajcie poleceń lidera, które podczas jazdy wyświetlane są na środku ekranu.
◾️ Lider, który prowadzi grupę podświetlony jest na żółto.
◾️ Starajcie się jechać blisko lidera, starając się go nie wyprzedać i nie wyjeżdżać przed bramę, która informuje Was że jedziecie za szybko.
Proszę stosujcie się do poleceń lidera!
Szczególnie jeśli lider zauważy że grupa się porwała.

◾️Jeśli chcecie sprawić sobie już teraz naszą nową teamową koszulkę ZTPL Cycling Club zapraszamy bezpośrednio na stronę https://inoni.cc/?s=ztpl&post_type=product
◾️Cała gama naszych koszulek oraz informacje o kolejnych Pre-Orderach znajdziecie na https://ztpl.cc/stroje-ztpl-cc/
◾️Nasze casualowe koszulki znajdziecie u Wintertala: https://wintertal.com/pl/133-ztplcc

[ENG]
☀️We’re now in the spring and summer season.☀️
Some of our events will stay in the calendar, but there may be times when our ride leaders won’t be there. If that happens, we’d really appreciate it if you could still ride according to the plan of the event.
Our ZTPL Cycling Club events will be back in autumn 2026.
For now — RIDE ON, enjoy the good weather, and if you can’t, just have a great time at our events.

We would like to invite you to ZTPL Cycling Club Coffee Ride.
In order to ride with Coffee Ride pace, please choose your group wisely.

Our rides are usually led by leaders who help keep the peloton in check. If there is no leader during the event, try to stick to the pace we agreed on when you joined the selected group.
◾️Group E is for total beginners with easy pace at around 1.3-1.7 wk/g
◾️Group D is a beginner group. The pace is calm, we learn how to ride in a Zwift peleton with 1.8-2.5 w/kg pace
◾️Group C is between group D and more advanced Group B. Pace 2.7-3.2 w/kg.
Important: If your FTP is around 2.5w/kg, this group will not be for you

◾️Please listen to the leader’s commands which are displayed in yellow during the whole ride. The leader who leads the group is highlighted in yellow. Please don’t pass the leader.
◾️Please follow the leader instructions! Especially if the leader notices that the group has been swept away. The leader is highlighted in yellow.
◾️At the and wait for the leader’s command: GOGOGO and start your sprint. Enjoy!

◾️If you would like to get yourself our ZTPL Cycling Club team jersey please visit our shop https://inoni.cc/en/?s=ztpl&post_type=product
or you can order our Casual T-Shirt and Hoodies https://wintertal.com/en/133-ztplcc

description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (ZWIFTEUR GHOST TEAM )
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by ZWIFTEUR GHOST TEAM (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/1401769a-6f9e-479e-902f-fd4c8752811d).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

name: Steed Cycles Stay at Home Workout
description: The Steed Cycles Stay at Home Workout is the perfect 'sweet-spot' workout to maintain your cycling fitness while outdoor group riding is restricted.  After a short warm-up, the Steed Cycles ride leader will ramp up to 'club pace' of 3 watts/kg, slowing down briefly every once every lap of the course to regroup.  Riders are asked to maintain club riding etiquette -- please keep a tight peloton and stay with the rider leader.  After 60 minutes, the pace will be 'riders choice' where riders can stretch their legs and race to the finish, or cool down or just hang with the group.  Enjoy!

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

name: USMeS - Sunday Endurance Ride [C] 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
description:
Sunday Endurance Ride 2.2–2.8
90 Minutes of Strong, Steady Miles

Close out your week with the Sunday Endurance Ride, a solid C-paced session immediately following the Sunday Climbers ride and is built to boost your fitness and sharpen your endurance. Whether you’re chasing segment PRs or just logging steady time in the saddle, this ride has something for everyone.

Pace: 2.2–2.8 w/kg
Average: ~2.5 w/kg overall
Beacons: Yellow beacon sets the pace + Red beacon to support riders off the back. Sometimes we let Red off his leash though... so be prepared for that
Fence: In use to keep the group tight—we’ll drop it for sprints and segments, but regroup quickly

Ride Format:

    • Consistent endurance pacing
    • Sprints and segments called out in advance—chase your PRs!
    • Regroup behind the beacon after each effort
    • Friendly, supportive vibe with plenty of wheels to help if you drift back

Expectations:
 Hold steady tempo between efforts
 Respect the fence when active
 Use sprints to push yourself, then quickly fall back into formation

If you’re looking for a well-paced endurance ride to finish your week strong, Sunday Endurance Ride is the place to be.

Please join our Discord https://discord.gg/uyE2eUh4yE and join our Zwift Club and FB by searching “US Military eSports”

#conqueringlimits #challengeaccepted

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Cheesecake for Breakfast
description:
Cheesecake for Breakfast – 100km Endurance Ride

Event Overview:

Join us for the “Cheesecake for Breakfast” 100km endurance ride! Whether you're looking
for a relaxed, social spin or a high-energy challenge, this ride offers something for everyone.
Upon completion of the ride, you will also unlock the Cheesecake Kit.

Ride Options:

• The Cake Crusaders (C Pace Group):
A steady, social ride at 2.7-3.4 w/kg. Perfect for team spirit, casual conversation,
and wrapping up your weekend or starting your week on a positive note.
• The Bombers (B Chase Group):
A more intense ride at 3.5–4.5 w/kg. Starting 12 minutes after the Crusaders, this
group is for those who enjoy chasing and pushing their limits. Often reaches A-pace!

Live ZOOM Call:
Experience the ride with an IRL vibe by joining our live ZOOM call during the event.
• Meeting ID: 870 0212 9082
• Passcode: eatcake

Our Vibe:

Both groups emphasize positivity, respect, and teamwork. Whether you're here for fun or
fitness, you’ll find a welcoming community.

We hope to see you there!

name: VirtuSlo 4Endurance Race - Flat is Fast
description:
Welcome to our VirtuSlo 4Endurance Race. 
The Rules are simple: finish as fast as possible and have Fun!


PEN -> Zwift Racing Score
Pen A / 700 - 1000
Pen B / 570 - 700
Pen C / 460 - 570
Pen D / 350 - 460
Pen E / 0 - 350

RULES AND RACE Settings
- No Powerups
- No TT Bikes
- Participants must be registered on zwiftpower.com to be included in the results
- Smart trainer or Power Meter required.
- Heart Rate Monitor required.
- No Indoor Bikes without Controlled Resistance (Cadence=Power)
- Sticky Watts or unusual pedalling technique to get an unrealistic advantage will result in DQ

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS
- Zwift Activities must be saved PUBLIC for verification.
- Elite Level Performances above the limits stated below must be supported by DUAL POWER Recordings uploaded to Zwiftpower and with margin off error of less than 4%. Outside KOM efforts on Strava can be used for verification of Dual Power analysis.
*MEN: above 4.8W/kg or 350W for 20min, 5.5W/kg or 425W for 5min, 9W/kg for 1min.
**WOMEN: above 4.5W/kg or 280W for 20min, 5W/kg or 325w for 5min, 7.5W/kg for 1min.

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER?
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Zwiftpower results: questions, complaints, etc., please contact us https://virtuslo.cc/

#VirtuSlo #4Endurance

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter
accessExpression:
(profile.playerType == "BROADCASTER") ||
(subgroup.label == 1 && scoring.current >= 390 && scoring.current <= 650) ||
(subgroup.label == 2 && scoring.current >= 270 && scoring.current < 510) ||
(subgroup.label == 3 && scoring.current >= 160 && scoring.current < 390) ||
(subgroup.label == 4 && scoring.current < 270) ||
(subgroup.label == 5 && scoring.current < 160)

name: TRON CHASERS GUIDE TO THE GALAXY [1.8-2.4 WKG] CAT D
description:
Welcome to our GXY TRON FEST AND BADGE HUNT EVENT!
A weekly event that brings you the best of both worlds, more metres towards that elusive Tron Bike and to tick off
those pesky event badges all at a pace that is both achievable and enjoyable !
Depending on the course chosen the ride leader may decide to operate both beacons at different power
points. This cool idea gives riders the chance to choose to ride with the yellow beacon at a higher power or chill with
the red beacon on a lower power within the Cat D power parameters.
The ride maybe set to duration or laps depending on the required event that day so be sure to check this each week and
make sure it works for you!
GALAXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed, we make each ride individual and fun

soon and are here to help you achieve your goals.

via our Facebook link or by the email below:

https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit || Advanced
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Neon After Party
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER?

name: ZZRC Ride With Friends 1.3-1.5
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Ride With Friends 1.5. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 1.3-1.5. on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be 1.5

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .

We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel
https://discord.gg/teHeHABDHc

TOP TIPS
1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!
2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone
3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: TBR Power Up
description:
TBR Power Up is an hour long, all category friendly interval session. There are two intervals at approximately 2.5 watts/kg and one open watts interval.
We recover at our base pace of 1.8 wkg for 10 minutes between each interval.

We roll out at 1.8wkg to group up. Interval 1 is 7.5 minutes at 2.5 w/kg. Interval 2 is 10 minutes at 2.5w/kg. The final interval is The Wild Goose Race with no w/kg limit for 10 minutes to really test your mettle.

We don't use the fence for this ride as we believe all riders should exercise self-control and respect the ride leader by riding with the yellow beacon at all times. Your co-operation helps us provide everyone with a fun, supportive and inclusive ride.
Follow TBR at thebigring.cc to learn more about our events, read ride reports and leave feedback.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Chill 'n' Spin Sunday Ride
description:
It’s time to join Team CLS for our Sunday Chill ‘n’ Spin ride… and UNLOCK our awesome Team CLS Kit.

Spin the legs and enjoy some banter. While stronger riders may choose to use this event as a recovery ride, this will also be an opportunity to get in a solid ride at mid D cat pace.

The focus is on riding with the bunch around the beacon as closely as possible. We see this ride as being a great event for riders that are:
- New to zwift, or have not tried a group ride before
- Recovering from injury
- Looking for a controlled group ride at D cat pace
- Easing back on the intensity and want a ride with plenty of chat.

Like all our events please make sure you can hold the above pace as we cannot reduce this at the expense of other riders although we will help to tow you back for dropouts etc.

WARNING - Please only attempt sneaky sprints on this ride if you feel your legs are sufficiently warmed up to do so. Sudden high intensity efforts on cold muscles is not advised. 


WEEKLY PROGRAM
Mon  18:50 UK - Team CLS Racing - Pace ‘n’ Race: Recon and Activation 
Tues 18:50 UK - This ‘n’ That - Endurance Training
Wed  18:50 UK - Ride ‘n’ Explore Club Rideout
Thur 18:50 UK - High ‘n’ Low - Interval Training
Sat  08:50 and 14:45 UK - Base ‘n’ Banter Endurance ride
Sun  18:50 UK - Chill 'n' Spin - Weekend Social


TEAM CLS LINKS
Check out www.teamclscc.co.uk ...find out about our club, current events and leaders. 

Facebook Club Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclscc
Facebook Racing Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclsracing
Zwift Power Team - https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1426



TEAM | CLS - Training | Racing | Community

name: Wakey-Wakey Kiwi Crew
description:
Join Johan Oelofse and the awesome Crew on the ride created by the legendary Greg "Coach Hendy" Henderson for this Kiwi group ride with a 10 min blast at the finish. Similar to the Thursday Kiwi Crew Ride. we'll be at 2.0-2.5 w per kg cruising for 35min and jam for the last 10 mins.

#kiwicrew

-----------------------------------------------------------------

Kiwi Crew Riot Race on Monday morning UTC
https://www.zwift.com/events/view/5031758

Kiwi Crew Riot Race
Event Description

This race is a Handicap Chaser / Chop style race with D Cat going first then C-B-A with time gaps in between. The goal is to work as a Team to reach the finish first to claim line honours, can D stay away or will the other categories chase them down before the finish?

Now one of the established major weekly races on the Zwift Calendar every Monday night so get your week off in the best way! the Riot Race sees a regular Crew battling for line honours every week. Pacer Group Auto Cat Join is on and make sure you have a Zwift Power account (www.zwiftpower.com) to be included in the official results!

* Everyone with a Power Meter and / or Smart Trainer is welcome to join.
* New cadence rules in play.
* Winners must have their Strava account linked and are subject to verification checks / requests.

Make sure you check out our other 2 rides, the Kiwi Crew Ride Thursday's at 6:30pm NZ, and the Wakey-Wakey ride, Monday's at 6:00am NZ . Come and join in the fun!

https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftnz

#kiwicrew

name: Bikealicious Climbalicious
description: We are a friendly gathering of cycling enthusiasts. We ride for 120 minutes, and for no less than 20 miles. You can ride with us 30 minutes or the full 2 hours. We ride a new climbing route every Sunday (edt) to keep the workout fresh and fun for all the zwifters. We ride with a rubber band to motivate all skill sets to stay together through challenging climbs resulting in an interactive and dynamic workout. Our group includes riders of all power levels. We welcome all riders.

name: HerD Beginner Races
description:
Where do you go to try racing or to grow a little better with your racing craft… Herd Beginner Races is set up to provide you with that opportunity by using Zwift Racing Scores (ZRS) with bandwidths that are set up so you’re competing with people of similarities and routes that enable you to challenge yourself and grow with racing. Our pens are set up as;

Pen A   ZRS  345-430

Pen B   ZRS 265-345

Pen C   ZRS 185-265

Pen D   ZRS 115-185

Pen E   ZRS 0-115

If you’re new to racing please take Zwift’s recommendation in blue.

Each week HBR races will have a different focus; racing on a flat route, rolling/undulating with a small climbs, and a route that is commonly used in many Zwift racing events. In addition, the last race of the month will be Tick-Tock so that you can monitor your performance.

name: GTR - Alpe Du Zwift Race
description:
Race the full Road to Sky route and finish with a climb up Zwift's most famous road, the Alpe Du Zwift. 

Race in pace categories A-D. 

This race is hosted by the Good Time Riders, GTR, an online bunch of friends from across the world who enjoy riding, racing hard, and generally having a good time together. Everyone is very welcome, you can find us on the Companion App, Facebook and Discord.

name: GardaBikeHotel Social Ride
description:
Welcome to Garda Bike Hotel social ride! We are a Bike Hotel on beautiful Lake Garda, Italy. We are specialized in cycling vacations and when here with us, our guests will experience the culture and beauty of our location, the breath taking riding routes, the local cuisine and the wine tasting. We also offer combined tours to other iconic cycling destinations including Stelvio, Dolomites, Piedmont, Adriatic Coast, Rome, Tuscany and Lake Como.

The aim of this inclusive Zwift ride is to have fun, be friendly and connect with our growing community around the world. All of our rides will be led by one of our friendly professional cycling guide. The route will change every week and we will try to keep the ride at around 2.0 watts per kg to make sure everyone can join in and enjoy the fun. We are an official PINARELLO rental centre and on our Zwift rides we will be riding a beautiful Pinarello Dogma F12!

To find out more about Garda Bike Hotel, please visit our website at https://www.gardabikehotel.com/en

Facebook: Garda Bike Hotel
Instagram: gardabikehotel

name: Know No Bounds Ride
description:
Welcome to the #KnowNoBounds Ride! A ride where we explore the boundaries (or lack thereof) on Zwift.
The ride will begin with 30 minutes at 3 w/kg, utilizing the fence to keep riders together. At the end of the 30 minutes, riders are free to do as they please:
they can stay together and explore the chosen course for the day, or they can head off to the nearest mountain with friends and race it!

When the 30 minute "fenced" portion of the ride is over, we will #KnowNoBounds. Have bike, will travel.
If needed, you will be able to turn to ride in opposite direction on the circuit to reconnect with the group quicker (if you need restroom or are dropped),
and most importantly, the group is not limited to the in-game courses. We can choose to go up a mountain, along a riverside, or any road we want to!

We’re on a mission: to set everyday cyclists free to explore without limits. And we’re going by bike.

Community and camaraderie in cycling is everything: group chat on this ride is highly encouraged!

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: ZSUN Sunday Social Ride
description:
ZSUN’s flagship Sunday ride since 2016, this long-standing group event offers a friendly, supportive experience with a strong sense of community. Choose the pen that suits your pace and enjoy the shared route experience.

Pen C starts first (2.4–2.6 w/kg), with Pen D (1.5–1.7 w/kg) following shortly after – giving riders the chance to drop back and find their sweet spot.

🗓️
Sundays | 60–120 mins | 2 pens

🚩
Pen C (2.4–2.6 w/kg) | CAT C

🚩
Pen D (1.5–1.7 w/kg) | CAT D

👥
Multiple leaders & sweepers

💬
Friendly chat + Discord optional

🎁
Ride and unlock the ZSUN kit

⚠️
Fence may be used to keep the group together

A great way to end your week with a steady effort and great company.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Rhino Crash Chase Race
description:
🏁 Rhino Crash Chase Race – Team Up, Stay Away, Win Big!
Ready to flip the script on solo racing? In the Rhino Chase, it’s all about teamwork within your category to outpace the others and reach the line first. Set aside rivalries and ride smart—your group’s success depends on it.

🚴 Staggered Category Start:
Category D kicks off first, followed by C, then B, and finally A.
The goal? Stay away and avoid being caught by the chasing categories!

📊 Race Format:
Standard Zwift categories with category enforcement in effect.
Weekly adjustments to time gaps and distances based on course selection to keep finishes tight and thrilling.

⚙️ Requirements:
Power Meter required to enter the pen.
Heart Rate Monitor mandatory for official results.

🎙️ Join the Conversation: Hop into the Rhino Discord (Public Crash voice channel) where we’ll be calling time gaps and cheering each other on. Everyone’s welcome!

https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Times Square Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

name: HCT Unleashed : Sunday Service
description:
Welcome to HCT Unleashed: Sunday Service.

The week is almost over. Have you paid your dues to the Cycling Gods? This is your final chance to empty the tank and find redemption in a short, sharp Sunday evening sufferfest.

The format is a chaotic mass start, with all categories unleashed at once. But here's the twist: while you can work with—or against—anyone on the course, your official results are only against riders in your own category.

Use the bunch to your advantage. Bridge a gap with a stronger rider or launch a late attack. It’s all part of the game. Join us every week for 20-40 minutes of tactical, high-octane racing.

If you like racing with HCT or are just looking for a racing community to get involved with, join the team on Zwift Power - ""Herts County Triathletes"" https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=19017 - and Captain Dan will be in touch to get you set up with the Discord Server. 
On the otherhand if you prefer some less intense involvement HCT also host a Z2 ride @ 3.15pm (GMT) on Saturdays. You can also join the Club on Zwift Companion App, and find us on YouTube, Facebook and Instagram.

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: PACK Run Hill Training: Alpe du Zwift
description:
This event will start on the Road to Sky route and end just 3k up the Alpe climb. There will be a 5k lead in of 0%-1% until we reach the start of the segment and then runners will set their treadmills to 8.5%-9% (depending on TM limitations) and "run" an additional 3km. The average pace to begin will be 10kph, but at 8.5%, it will quickly drop to around 8kph. Each week, there will be an after party, where you can test your ability to get closer and closer to the top. Ultimately, we are preparing you to reach the top, but in order to do that you must transverse the Alpe - 12.2km (7.6 miles) long with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain (approx 3,400 feet) at an average gradient of 8.5%.

This may sound like a daunting task, but that's why we are here. We want to support you in your journey to the top.

If you'd like Zwift to record your elevation, you will either need a treadmill that connects directly to zwift, Runn Smart Treadmill Sensor by North Pole Engineering, or an app like Runcline.

Are you ready?

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

Strava Club:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift-pack-runners
Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Good Time Riders GTR)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by Good Time Riders (GTR) (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/10bd6884-1218-4698-af77-9fda887d4aa7).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

name: Workout 3 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 3: Cadence Mastery
Dial in your cadence and watch everything else fall into place. This workout will have you switching between high and low cadence, all while keeping power steady, so outdoor riding feels more composed.

name: Stage 3: Pas Racing: Yumezi Grit
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes — including the new Gravel Mountain map — in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Redrock Loop
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off at Redrock Loop on the brand-new Gravel Mountain map. A fast, punchy gravel test to set the tone. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Redrock Loop Reverse
Date: April 27 – May 3

Back for more on the brand new Gravel Mountain map. One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: TFC Group Group Ride Sub 2
description:
The TFC ride is open to all riders on Zwift, if you have not already, please opt in to Zwiftpower or you will not be included in the Zwiftpower results screen.
Please follow the yellow beacon who will be the designated ride lead and obey any instructions. Please ensure no TT bikes to benefit from the group draft.
C Group

The sweep team will try to pick up any dropped riders and bring them back to the group if they can hold 2.2-2.3wkg.
During the last lap the ride leader will call for a race to the flag. Wait for the ride leaders’ instructions before you go.

D Group

The D Group will be a Sub 2 W/Kg. The sweep team will pick up dropped riders and bring them back into the main pack. Remember this is the ride average so there will be times the group is slightly above the advertised W/Kg and times when it is below. Listen to the ride lead to ensure everybody is in the main group.

BEWARE - If you do drop off the back of the group you will need to ride at a considerable higher pace to get back to the group. The Sweep teams are there to tow you back on if you do drop off,
We will be on Discord: https://discord.gg/yU6Gkcu
If you’re interested in joining Team TFC please check out: www.facebook.com/groups/teamtfc

name: RollCo: Night Shift
description:
Night Shift is Rolling Collectives after-hours ritual race.

When the lights drop and the noise fades, this is where racing gets raw. Short, sharp courses. Mixed terrain.  No hiding behind long climbs or safe pacing. From the first pedal stroke, it’s on.
Night Shift is built for riders who race on feel and instinct. Attacks come hard, selections happen fast, and hesitation costs you. You don’t wait for the race to start, you make it.

This is late-night racing for those who like it intense, imperfect and alive. All in, after dark.

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: The Grind Finale
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

This session aims to replicate the demands of a mountain bike ride / event. 

Mountain biking generally involves far more variations in effort compared with road riding - so plenty of surges and time spent being over geared! We also spend a lot of time on and off the power, so short spells going  hard to get over an obstacle, followed by a freewheel or very low power on a descent or little dip.

We touch upon all of these aspects in todays session, as we start off alternating every 30 seconds between riding hard at a low cadence, and riding easy - as if we are riding through an undulating single track. Then we make a ramp in effort, which finishes with a 30 second near max effort up a steep, rocky banking!

WORKOUT DESIGNER

Matt Rowe - Heavily involved in cycling for the past 25 years, Matt represented Great Britain at the Junior World Championships before graduating to the pro ranks. Matt draws on his extensive race experience to help amateur cyclists around the world achieve their personal goals. 

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop. 

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

Please stay together as a group around the Beacon and feel free to get involved in the chat💬. It is a group ride - NOT a race!

name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Sunday Funday p/b TBR
description:
Come hang with some friendly peeps on TBR’s D-friendly ride! We roll together in a big, happy bunch at an average 2.0w/kg pace.

We don’t use the fence in favor of control and respect for the ride leader and listed pace. Our rides are fence free and whinge free, if you stick to the leader’s Yellow Beacon like a bear to honey you will enjoy the benefits of a group draft along with lots of friendly banter. We want you to have a happy spin on the bike!

We may be The Big Ring and known for powerful riders, but our tent is also big and we encourage riders comfortable at a social pace to meet our friendly crew and learn about the TBR Effect. We are all about supporting riders in a friendly environment and helping people feel comfortable gradually working up to a faster pace. At TBR, we love to help riders look inside themselves and discover the hidden power within.

Follow The Big Ring (TBR) at www.thebigring.cc

name: The HERD Sunday Sip and Spin
description:
Hello ladies of Zwift! Come join the Herd ladies as we explore all the flats, hills, and everything in between on this banded ladies only ride.
As long as you keep spinning, at whatever pace you want, we will stay together. Want to tootle along at .5 w/kg? It’s all good. Want to get a high-powered workout in at 3 w/kg? Awesome! You’ll pull us along! Want to do intervals? That works too!
The only time we won’t do a banded ride is when we’re having a sprint practice ride. Otherwise, together we shall stay!
So, grab your favorite drink to Sip and come join us as we Spin further, climb higher and get stronger together!

Don't forget to join us on Discord. Download the app from https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV and use the “Ride Audio” channel.
Check out our website: theherd.club for a link to all our rides, Facebook, Strava and our sister racing league.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home
